text: How can you indicate that your vessel is distress?

options.text: A) Sounding five or more short and rapid blasts on the whistle
B) Continuously sounding the fog whistle
C) Displaying a large red flag
D) Displaying three black balls in a vertical line

explanation: Distress signals at sea are defined by COLREGs. In addition to visual signals, continuous sounding of the ship's fog whistle is a recognized audible distress signal that indicates the vessel is in urgent need of assistance.
